ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for kuih lapis; A0010X (Cakes, pastries and sweet pies) is the closest parent category for this steamed layered sweet cake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for kuih lapis; 25590003 (Sweet food) is the closest parent concept available.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Rainbow Lapis Kueh is one piece, weighing approximately 50 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Foods — Rainbow Lapis Kueh
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Rainbow Lapis Kueh (F11090050). Saturated fat estimated at 4.2g/100g based on coconut milk fat composition typical of this preparation; potassium estimated at 55mg/100g from ingredient composition as HPB does not report this field for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — rice-flour and tapioca-based steamed cakes analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Rainbow Lapis Kueh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 (medium) is estimated by analogy with glutinous rice flour and tapioca-based steamed cakes reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) International tables of GI and GL values, which lists similar rice-flour steamed confections in the GI range 60–72. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 31.3 × 0.50) / 100 = 10.2g for a 50g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9g/100g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.3g/100g) and low potassium (~55mg/100g) contributes to empty-calorie intake and may promote vascular inflammation.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit Rainbow Lapis Kueh to very small, infrequent portions due to its high sugar content, negligible fibre, and low potassium, which together provide no cardiovascular benefit and may worsen metabolic risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic index (~65) combined with 31.3g carbohydrate/100g from refined glutinous rice and sago flours, with only 0.3g dietary fibre providing no glycaemic buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Rainbow Lapis Kueh because its high refined-carbohydrate load, medium-high GI, and negligible fibre will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.2g/100g) from coconut milk combined with high added sugar (9g/100g) and negligible fibre may contribute to dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of Rainbow Lapis Kueh, as the combination of coconut-milk saturated fat and high sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and promote low-grade vascular inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rainbow Lapis Kueh (Kuih Lapis Pelangi) is a traditional Malay and Singaporean steamed layered cake distinguished by its vibrant alternating coloured layers. It is made from glutinous rice flour, sago flour, coconut milk, and sugar, steamed layer by layer to produce a soft, chewy, and slightly springy texture with a rich, sweet coconut flavour. The rainbow colouring is achieved using food-grade dyes applied to each individual layer before steaming. It is widely consumed as a snack or dessert and is a common sight at Malay festive celebrations, morning markets (pasar pagi), and Singaporean hawker stalls. Nutritionally, it provides 174 kcal per 100g, is high in refined carbohydrates (31.3g/100g), contains moderate fat primarily from coconut milk (5g/100g, of which approximately 4.2g is saturated), and is low in protein (1g/100g) and dietary fibre (0.3g/100g). Its medium glycemic index (~65) and high carbohydrate density make it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The food is gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal.
common_user_queries: How is Rainbow Lapis Kueh made?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient data (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are directly sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11090050), an approved primary regional source. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008) peer-reviewed tables for comparable glutinous rice flour steamed cakes, which is the approved GI source. Saturated fat and potassium are estimated from ingredient composition and documented accordingly.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient, and moisture data for this traditional steamed kueh are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11090050) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation as of 2026. Potassium_mg (55mg/100g) is estimated from ingredient composition (coconut milk, glutinous rice flour, sago flour) as HPB does not report potassium for this item. Saturated_fat_g (4.2g/100g) is estimated from the known fatty acid profile of coconut milk at the reported fat level of 5g/100g. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients. GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on comparable glutinous rice flour and tapioca-based steamed cakes in Atkinson et al. (2008) International tables of GI and GL values (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); no direct GI measurement for Rainbow Lapis Kueh is available in the University of Sydney GI Database.
